data.title: MGM Resorts reaches new employment agreement with CEO & President
data.description:
• MGM Resorts (NYSE:MGM) said on Thursday that it has reached a new employment agreement with CEO & President Bill Hornbuckle through December 31, 2028. 
  • As part of his new employment contract, the company has also agreed to offer Hornbuckle an advisory agreement at the end of the term to assist with its integrated resort project in Osaka, Japan until its opening.

data.title: Twin Vee PowerCats announces proposed public offering of common stock
data.description:
• Twin Vee PowerCats Co. (NASDAQ:VEEE) on Thursday said it intends to offer to sell shares of common stock in an underwritten public offering.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering primarily for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • VEEE -35.16% after hours to $4.83.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CeriBell GAAP EPS of -$0.36 beats by $0.08, revenue of $20.5M beats by $1.2M
data.description:
• CeriBell press release (NASDAQ:CBLL): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.36 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $20.5M (+42.4% Y/Y) beats by $1.2M.
  • Ceribell is raising its revenue guidance for FY2025 to a range of $83M to $87M, representing growth of ~27% to 33% over the company’s prior year revenue.

data.title: Yelp GAAP EPS of $0.36 beats by $0.03, revenue of $359M beats by $5.89M
data.description:
• Yelp press release (NYSE:YELP): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.36 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $359M (+7.9% Y/Y) beats by $5.89M.
  • Shares -0.14%.

  • Net Income increased by 72% year over year to $24 million, reflecting a 7% margin

    Adjusted EBITDA grew 32% year over year to $85 million, reflecting a 24% margin1

    Updates 2025 Net Revenue outlook to the range of $1.465 billion to $1.485 billion; and Adjusted EBITDA2 outlook to the range of $345 million to $365 million

  • FY revenue consensus of $1.47B

data.title: Clean Energy Fuels Non-GAAP EPS of $0.01 beats by $0.17, revenue of $103.8M beats by $5.82M
data.description:
• Clean Energy Fuels press release (NASDAQ:CLNE): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.01 beats by $0.17.
  • Revenue of $103.8M (+0.1% Y/Y) beats by $5.82M.

data.title: Pinterest GAAP EPS of $0.01 beats by $0.02, revenue of $854.99M beats by $8.89M
data.description:
• Pinterest press release (NYSE:PINS): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.01 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $854.99M (+15.5% Y/Y) beats by $8.89M.
  • Global Monthly Active Users ("MAUs") increased 10% year over year to 570 million.
  • For Q2 2025, we expect revenue to be in the range of $960 million to $980 million, representing 12% - 15% growth year over year. We expect Q2 2025 Adjusted EBITDA* to be in the range of $217 million to $237 million.
  • Shares +10%.

data.title: Expedia Non-GAAP EPS of $0.40 beats by $0.04, revenue of $2.99B misses by $20M
data.description:
• Expedia press release (NASDAQ:EXPE): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.40 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $2.99B (+3.5% Y/Y) misses by $20M.
  • Booked room nights grew 6% in the first quarter year-over-year amid softened travel demand within and into the U.S.
  • Total gross bookings grew 4% in the first quarter. B2C gross bookings grew 1% and B2B gross bookings grew 14% given the segment’s greater international exposure.
  • Lodging gross bookings grew 5% year-over-year in the first quarter; hotel bookings were up 6%, driven by resilience at B2B and Brand Expedia.
  • Revenue grew 3% in the quarter, led by our B2B and Advertising businesses, which grew 14% and 20%, respectively.
  • Shares -5%.

data.title: Copa Holdings signals 7%-8% capacity growth and 21%-23% operating margin for 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Copa Holdings (CPA) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Pedro Heilbron highlighted the company's strong start to 2025, achieving a 23.8% operating margin in Q1. He attributed this to low fuel unit costs, industry-leading on-time performance, and a passenger-friendly product. Capacity grew by 9.5% year-over-year, with passenger traffic increasing by 10.1% and a load factor of 86.4%.
  • Heilbron announced the launch of services to San Diego, California, and two cities in Argentina, Salta and Tucuman, further solidifying Panama’s position as a hub for travel in the Americas. Wingo, Copa’s low-cost carrier subsidiary, added a new domestic Colombian route and is set to expand its fleet to ten 737-800s by year-end.
  • CFO Peter Donkersloot reported a net profit of $176.8 million for Q1, or $4.28 per share, representing a net margin of 19.7%. He emphasized the company’s robust liquidity, with over $1.3 billion in cash and investments, excluding $600 million in pre-delivery deposits for new aircraft. The adjusted net debt-to-EBITDA ratio remains low at 0.5x.
  • Donkersloot confirmed that Copa exercised options for six additional Boeing 737 MAX-8 aircraft for 2028 delivery, increasing its order book to 57 aircraft. The fleet is expected to reach 125 aircraft by the end of 2025.

Outlook

  • Management increased the 2025 operating margin guidance to 21%-23%, citing lower fuel costs and steady passenger demand. ASM capacity growth is forecasted at 7%-8% year-over-year.
  • CFO Donkersloot outlined expectations for an 86.5% load factor and unit revenues of $0.112 for the year. Fuel costs are projected at $2.40 per gallon.

Financial Results

  • Operating profit for Q1 was $213.8 million, corresponding to a 23.8% operating margin. Unit revenues (RASM) were $11.05, down 8.1% year-over-year due to a decrease in passenger yields.
  • Unit costs excluding fuel (CASMx) improved by 4.3% year-over-year to $5.08, driven by reduced sales and distribution expenses and disciplined management of headcount and overhead.
  • The company reported on-time performance of 90.8% and a completion factor of 99.9%.

Q&A

  • Savi Syth, Raymond James: Asked about U.S. point-of-sale strength and regional differences. CEO Heilbron noted steady demand without material changes recently but acknowledged limited visibility beyond two to three months.
  • Duane Pfennigwerth, Evercore ISI: Inquired about distribution cost savings. CFO Donkersloot revealed ongoing savings from the NDC channel, with 85% of sales now direct or via NDC.
  • Guilherme Mendes, JPMorgan: Asked about demand segmentation and regional RASM impacts. Heilbron stated business travel accounts for 20% of traffic, with Brazil showing yield weakness due to currency issues, while South America and other regions remain stable.
  • Michael Linenberg, Deutsche Bank: Questioned the impact of fuel cost assumptions on margins. Heilbron acknowledged potential margin improvement if fuel costs stay lower than forecasted.
  • Alberto Valerio, UBS: Asked about capacity adjustments amidst potential global aircraft delivery delays. Heilbron confirmed no significant changes to Copa's delivery schedule.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about competitive capacity and FX volatility impacts on yields, reflecting cautious sentiment. Queries also focused on demand trends and fleet utilization.
  • Management maintained a cautiously optimistic tone, emphasizing strong liquidity and operational flexibility. Heilbron cited Copa’s ability to shift capacity across markets as a buffer against regional demand fluctuations.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management raised operating margin guidance to 21%-23%, up from 20%-22% in the previous quarter, supported by lower fuel cost assumptions.
  • Capacity growth remains consistent at 7%-8%, while load factor expectations hold steady at 86.5%.
  • Analysts’ questions in the current quarter focused more on regional demand segmentation and competitive capacity pressures compared to broader market outlook discussions in Q4 2024.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted excess capacity in specific regions like Brazil and Central America as a risk factor impacting yields.
  • FX volatility in key markets such as Brazil continues to pose challenges, though management noted some recent stabilization.
  • Analysts expressed concerns about geopolitical risks, such as the ongoing suspension of flights to Venezuela, and their impact on the network.

Final Takeaway

Copa Holdings delivered a strong Q1 2025, marked by robust margins and disciplined cost control. Management raised the full-year operating margin guidance to 21%-23% and reaffirmed a 7%-8% capacity growth target. While competitive capacity and FX volatility present challenges, Copa’s strong liquidity, operational flexibility, and strategic initiatives, including network expansion, position it well for consistent performance in 2025.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Cogent communications sets 6-8% annual revenue growth target amid expanding wavelength services
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Cogent Communications Holdings, Inc. (CCOI) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Dave Schaeffer highlighted that the company achieved significant milestones in Q1 2025, including offering wavelength services in 883 data centers with capabilities for 10-gig, 100-gig, and 400-gig connections. Sequential wavelength revenue increased by 2.2%, with a total of $7.1 million for the quarter. The company anticipates capturing 25% of the North American wavelength market within three years.
  • IPv4 leasing revenue grew 14.8% sequentially to $14.4 million, supported by pricing increases. The average revenue per IPv4 address rose to $0.49, up from $0.30 at the start of the year.
  • Gross margin increased by 790 basis points year-over-year to 44.6%, driven by a reduction in cost of goods sold. SG&A expenses were impacted by seasonal factors but declined year-over-year.
  • The company repurchased approximately 100,000 shares for $5 million under its stock buyback program and announced a dividend increase to $1.01 per share, marking the 51st consecutive quarterly increase.
  • CFO Tad Weed reported Q1 2025 revenue of $247 million, with adjusted EBITDA of $68.8 million and a margin increase of 130 basis points to 27.8%.

Outlook

  • Management updated long-term annual revenue growth targets to 6-8% and anticipates an annual EBITDA margin expansion of 150 basis points. These targets are set as multi-year goals rather than quarterly or annual guidance.
  • The company expects to return to total top-line revenue growth by mid-Q3 2025 as the process of grooming undesirable Sprint revenues concludes.
  • Wavelength services are projected to reach 10,000 opportunities in the funnel by year-end 2025, with a provisioning capacity of 500 waves per month.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q1 2025 totaled $247 million. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $68.8 million, reflecting a sequential increase of $1.9 million.
  • Corporate revenue represented 44.9% of total revenue, declining year-over-year due to the continued grooming of low-margin off-net connections.
  • NetCentric revenue, accounting for 37.5% of total revenue, saw a slight sequential decline driven by adjustments in the T-Mobile agreement and foreign exchange impacts.
  • On-net revenue increased sequentially by 0.7% to $129.6 million, while off-net revenue decreased sequentially by 5.2%.
  • Monthly churn rates for on-net and off-net were reported at 1.4% and 2.2%, respectively.

Q&A

  • Greg Williams, TD Cowen, asked about dividend growth and the timeline to reach 500 monthly wavelength installations. CEO Schaeffer clarified that dividend increases are tied to leverage reduction, expected to begin in Q4 2025. He stated that the company is on track to achieve its monthly wavelength installation target by year-end.
  • Alex Waters, Bank of America, inquired about wavelength ARPU trends and data center monetization. CEO Schaeffer estimated ARPU to stabilize around $1,900 to $2,000 per wavelength and noted ongoing negotiations for data center deals, with completion expected in the coming months.
  • Walter Piecyk, LightShed, questioned the sequential growth in IPv4 addresses and sales funnel grooming for wavelengths. Management explained that price increases influenced address additions and outlined a 5% monthly funnel conversion rate.
  • Tim Horan, Oppenheimer, discussed competitive dynamics in the wavelength market. CEO Schaeffer highlighted Cogent's superior provisioning times, redundancy, and pricing advantages as key differentiators.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about the pace of corporate revenue recovery and the competitive landscape for wavelength services. While generally cautious, some analysts acknowledged the company's progress in addressing operational challenges.
  • Management expressed confidence during prepared remarks but faced pressing questions about specific growth initiatives and timelines during the Q&A. CEO Schaeffer emphasized the importance of execution and maintained a proactive stance on competitive threats.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue decreased from $252.3 million in Q4 2024 to $247 million in Q1 2025, reflecting ongoing adjustments in undesirable Sprint contracts and seasonal factors.
  • Adjusted EBITDA margin improved sequentially from 26.5% to 27.8%, driven by cost optimizations and higher-margin wavelength services.
  • Management tone remained consistent, with continued focus on growth initiatives, though slightly more cautious about external market conditions compared to the previous quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • The company highlighted potential risks related to macroeconomic conditions and slower-than-expected adoption of new services. Management intends to mitigate these risks through cost controls and expanded service offerings.
  • Analysts noted competitive pressures in the wavelength market and the impact of slower corporate revenue recovery on overall financial performance.

Final Takeaway

Cogent Communications aims to solidify its position in the wavelength market, projecting significant growth in service adoption and revenue expansion. Despite challenges in corporate revenue and competitive pressures, management's focus on strategic initiatives and operational efficiencies underpins confidence in achieving long-term growth targets.
